---
title: "Apple and Google Maps history"
author:
  - name: Ralph Straumann
    url: https://ralphstraumann.ch
date: "2026-04-12 12:31"
image: "apple-google-comparison.gif"
description: "Marcin Wichary curates Justin O'Beirne's series on the evolution of #Apple and #Google Maps' style, content, and #POI coverage over the years. Justin's articles are a few years old, but the insights on web #cartography and real-world use cases are still well worth reading if you work with (or even just use) #webmaps."
---

2<sup>nd</sup> degree recommendation: At (the also great) [Unsung][unsung], 
Marcin Wichary [highlights a series of carefully illustrated articles][article] 
by Justin O’Beirne that document and discuss the (sometimes surprisingly 
dynamic[^data-processes]) evolution of style and content of Apple and Google 
Maps over the years, for example about the [churn of POIs[^pois]][example].

Another interesting example is the [anecdote][anecdote] about firefighters' map 
use highlighted by Marcin. The articles are a few years old, but the insights 
are still relevant and interesting to read if you work with web maps.

![Comparison of Apple and Google Maps evolving POI content (source: [Justin O'Beirne](https://www.justinobeirne.com/a-year-of-google-maps-and-apple-maps))](apple-google-comparison.gif "Comparison of Apple and Google Maps evolving POI content (source: Justin O'Beirne)")

[^pois]: Points-of-interest.
[^data-processes]: Makes me wonder also about the underlying data-generating process.

[unsung]: https://unsung.aresluna.org/
[article]: https://unsung.aresluna.org/so-i-wrote-a-script-that-takes-monthly-screenshots-of-google-and-apple-maps/
[example]: https://www.justinobeirne.com/a-year-of-google-maps-and-apple-maps
[anecdote]: https://www.justinobeirne.com/fire-roads
